reasoning: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.40 (2/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.

concerns.analysis: The conversion results are incomplete. The output explicitly contains an entry stating '28 sections omitted for token budget'. Extensive content from the RQI T-CPR Program (Pages 7-27), Cardiac Arrest Case Review (Pages 29-30), BLS Responder Education (Page 31), D-RAK Performance Objectives (Page 34 intro), and D-RAK Payment Options (Page 36) is entirely absent from the output.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Multiple pages and sections including: RQI T-CPR Program Instructional Overview (Pages 7-17), Cardiac Arrest Case Review (Page 29), and Training Payment Options (Page 36).

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: Within the contact paragraph for Dan Jewiss on page 35, the output omits critical information. Specifically, the email address 'D.Jewiss@me.com' and the website URL 'www.TheRAKAcademy.com' are missing from the paragraph in element indices 467 and 469.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Please contact Dan Jewiss at D.Jewiss@me.com or 203-704-0581 with any questions or to host this training. www.TheRAKAcademy.com
concerns.output_quote: Please contact Dan Jewiss at ... or 203-704-0581 with any questions or to host this training.
concerns.location: Section containing reading_order 467 and 469, Page 35 content

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy in the Minutes section (starting at page 3) is broken. The section title is assigned level 1 (reading_order 14), but the subsequent major sections (Items 6, 7, 8) are assigned level 3 (reading_order 20, 25, 26) without an intervening level 2 heading. This represents a heading skip which hinders screen reader navigation.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The Minutes section contains major headings for items 1-11.
concerns.output_quote: Item 6 (Technology Update) is level 3 (reading_order 20) following an H1 (reading_order 14).

concerns.analysis: In the Minutes section (pages 3-6), the original document contains a single numbered list (items 1-11). The output disintegrates this structure: items 1-5 are placed in an unordered list (reading_order 16), items 6-8 are converted into H3 headings, and items 9-11 are rendered as standard paragraphs. This significantly changes the structural meaning and accessibility of the list.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Numbered list items 1 through 11 in the Minutes section.
concerns.output_quote: Section 16 (unordered list for 1-5), Reading Order 20 (H3 for 6), Reading Order 30 (paragraph for 9).

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: In the minutes for item 11 (Page 6), the output omits the word 'Course' from the end of the formal motion text. While minor, this is a wording change in a formal government record that slightly alters the specificity of the reference.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: ...registration for a second NENA Center Manager Certification Program (CMCP) Course.
concerns.output_quote: ...fund the course registration for a second NENA Center Manager Certification Program (CMCP).

concerns.analysis: At least one critical contact link is missing. On page 35 of the source (RAK Academy info), an email address 'D.Jewiss@me.com' is clearly present. In the output JSON, the text elements (reading_order 467 and 469) skip this email address entirely, leaving a gap in the sentence.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Please contact Dan Jewiss at D.Jewiss@me.com or 203-704-0581...
